title: chore(deps): update dependency containers/automation_images to v20230726
body:
[![Mend Renovate](https://app.renovatebot.com/images/banner.svg)](https://renovatebot.com)

This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Update | Change |
|---|---|---|
| [containers/automation_images](https://togithub.com/containers/automation_images) | major | `20230426t140447z-f38f37d12` -> `20230517t144652z-f38f37d12` |

---

comments.body:
One test is failing, https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4968184671895552/html/int-podman-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--podman-checkpoint-container-with-export--migration--and-ipc-host--1

@giuseppe PTAL look like a possible crun bug in f38/rawhide, f37 and debian seems to work fine.

Also selinux tests are failing on f38: https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4528380020785152/html/sys-podman-fedora-38-root-host-sqlite.log.html
@rhatdan PTAL
Thanks Paul for checking & pinging.  I managed to completely loose track of this PR :disappointed:
Added to https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18577 to see if this fixes both problems.
force-push: Attempted rebasing ontop of Dan's #18577 since it's CI went all green.
@rhatdan FWIW: rebased with your PR, seems we get a handful of checkpoint and SELinux test failures.
I don't see how https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18577 is relevant for the test failures?

> I don't see how https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18577 is relevant for the test failures?

Honestly I'm just throwing a bucket of golf-balls and hoping one lands in the hole.  I read Dan's comment above and thought I'd give it a try.  I should probably pull it out and rebase again, see if anything's changed from your assessment.

@giuseppe ping - Any feedback on [Paul's observation](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18612#issuecomment-1554581011) of a possible crun bug in [this `podman checkpoint container with export (migration) and --ipc host` failure](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5124176139255808/html/int-podman-fedora-38-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--podman-checkpoint-container-with-export--migration--and-ipc-host--1)?
are we using crun 1.8.5?  There was an issue in crun 1.8.4 with CRIU on Fedora that could have caused this problem
CI logs says `crun-1.8.4-1.fc38-x86_64` so no.

Thanks @giuseppe and @Luap99. Darn, so that means `1.8.5` wasn't in the updates-testing repo. at image-build time (a few weeks ago).  I see the [same test is failing on rawhide](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4842701162545152/html/int-podman-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html) as well, with `crun-1.8.4-1.fc39-x86_64`.  The [bodhi pages show `1.8.5` entries](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/?search=crun-1.8.5) from **last week**.  So it sounds like I simply need to build newer CI VM Images then...
...opened https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/281
Force-push: New CI VM images.

### Edited/Blocked Notification

Renovate will not automatically rebase this PR, because it does not recognize the last commit author and assumes somebody else may have edited the PR.

You can manually request rebase by checking the rebase/retry box above.

 ⚠ **Warning**: custom changes will be lost.
force-push: Rebased.

[All system tests failing](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5539173663244288) with:

```
Failed tests (3):
 - 410 podman selinux: container with label=disable
 - 411 podman selinux: privileged container
 - 416 podman selinux: pid=host
```

I did not look deeply, for now I'm assuming it's related to the fixes Dan's working on.
This looks suspiciously like the "need a new container-selinux" problem that was floating around 2-3 weeks ago? I thought that the required container-selinux version was already in stable?

> I thought that the required container-selinux version was already in stable?

Ya, I rebuilt these images to include that new version as per Dan's request in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18439  I believe there's a similar thing happening in buildah-land with https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4829 Though In both cases, I'm really only guessing :disappointed:
Can you double-check? In the failed-test log for f38 root I see container-selinux-2.**216**.0-1.fc38-noarch. On my f38 laptop I see **218**.

> Can you double-check?

Thanks for lending your eyeballs.  Honestly, I'm not surprised there's a newer one, there's been a lot of churn lately.  I think Dan was originally asking for `2.213`. But there was also some back/forth with me to enable `[CI:NEXT]` (here and in buildah) so he could test newer versions.  Either way, I can certainly update the CI VMs and see if it fixes the problem or even changes the symptoms...
...Opened **https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/282**
Force-push: [Updated to `c20230614t132754z-f38f37d13` CI VM images](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/282) (w/ container-selinux 2:2.218.0-1.fc38)

@Luap99 I'm seeing a scattering of [pasta test failures in the latest run](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5083225773572096).  Here's a sampeling:

[not ok 466 podman networking with pasta(1) - Local forwarder, IPv4](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4722583141089280/html/sys-podman-debian-13-rootless-host-boltdb.log.html) (maybe a flake)
```
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
         # #| FAIL: exit code is 1; expected 0
         # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
         # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
         # #|     FAIL: No answer from resolver
         # #| expected: =~ 1.0.0.127.in-addr.arpa
         # #|   actual:    \;\; connection timed out\; no servers could be reached
         # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

Then two similar failures for [not ok 452 podman networking with pasta(1) - IPv4 address assignment](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5989220536287232/html/sys-podman-fedora-38-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html).
```
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
         # #|     FAIL: Container {address,route} not matching configured value
```

There are a few others scattered about but I didn't look too deeply.
Those are new; @sbrivio-rh PTAL ^^

@rhatdan Despite updating images again (w/ container-selinux 2:2.218.0-1.fc38) I'm still seeing (basically) the same three failures.  I just wanted to confirm these are the failures you're addressing (somewhere)?

```
 - 410 podman selinux: container with label=disable
 - 411 podman selinux: privileged container
 - 416 podman selinux: pid=host
```

> Those are new; @sbrivio-rh PTAL ^^

Sorry, currently traveling, give me a couple of days...
No worries, this isn't exactly a young PR anyway :grin:
force-push: rebased.

@sbrivio-rh In case it helps these can be reproduced locally:
 - 454 podman networking with pasta(1) - IPv4 address assignment
 - 460 podman networking with pasta(1) - IPv4 default route assignment

bisected to `cc9d1675 conf, pasta: With --config-net, copy all addresses by default`

full log for reference:
```
git bisect start
# status: waiting for both good and bad commits
# bad: [429e1a7e71ad9020f0e53bc467986c55bf5c0e38] conf: Fix erroneous check of ip6->gw
git bisect bad 429e1a7e71ad9020f0e53bc467986c55bf5c0e38
# status: waiting for good commit(s), bad commit known
# good: [b10b983fbd00634e275083c37446a538dbff0dbe] fedora: Adjust path for SELinux policy and interface file to latest guidelines
git bisect good b10b983fbd00634e275083c37446a538dbff0dbe
# good: [940bd3eff922c46ac280136e3674ee70ea40f08e] passt: Fix error check for signal(), improve error messages
git bisect good 940bd3eff922c46ac280136e3674ee70ea40f08e
# good: [468f19a85268ac122c2a05cf7a8552eebbbb1bbe] conf: --config-net option is for pasta mode only
git bisect good 468f19a85268ac122c2a05cf7a8552eebbbb1bbe
# good: [e89da3cf03b2e301504f0d9c45db8e6056e66beb] netlink: Add functionality to copy addresses from outer namespace
git bisect good e89da3cf03b2e301504f0d9c45db8e6056e66beb
# bad: [9f61c5b68b0958e0c7735657ed3842866e45ea47] passt.h: Fix description of pasta_ifi in struct ctx
git bisect bad 9f61c5b68b0958e0c7735657ed3842866e45ea47
# bad: [cc9d16758be69c0ca16eb7f11b31a07945cf427e] conf, pasta: With --config-net, copy all addresses by default
git bisect bad cc9d16758be69c0ca16eb7f11b31a07945cf427e
# first bad commit: [cc9d16758be69c0ca16eb7f11b31a07945cf427e] conf, pasta: With --config-net, copy all addresses by default
```
The routes are completely empty when a gateway is set.

> @sbrivio-rh In case it helps these can be reproduced locally:
> 
>     * 454 podman networking with pasta(1) - IPv4 address assignment
> 
>     * 460 podman networking with pasta(1) - IPv4 default route assignment
> 
> 
> bisected to `cc9d1675 conf, pasta: With --config-net, copy all addresses by default`

Thanks, yes, this just saved me some time. I had started having a look yesterday but didn't bisect yet.

> > @sbrivio-rh In case it helps these can be reproduced locally:
> > ```
> > * 454 podman networking with pasta(1) - IPv4 address assignment
> > 
> > * 460 podman networking with pasta(1) - IPv4 default route assignment
> > ```
> > 
> >   
> > bisected to `cc9d1675 conf, pasta: With --config-net, copy all addresses by default`
> 
> Thanks, yes, this just saved me some time. I had started having a look yesterday but didn't bisect yet.

Fixes are rather obvious, I'm now checking a couple of other issues before posting a series, but I think we should have this fixed later today or tomorrow.

Issues with tests:
`  podman networking with pasta(1) - IPv4 address assignment`
`  podman networking with pasta(1) - IPv4 default route assignment`

are fixed in Fedora 38 package [update](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2023-c0b38e1f86) `passt-0^20230625.g32660ce-1.fc38`, corresponding to (future) Debian update `0.0~git20230625.32660ce-1`. Note that the current package from Debian 12 is not affected as it didn't contain the offending change.

Force-push: Long-shot check that a rebase doesn't alter anything.  If the problem is fixed in updated packages, I'll need to build fresh CI VM images.

> Note that the current package from Debian 12 is not affected as it didn't contain the offending change.

The debian CI VM images are all based on SID, so this current set bumps us up to Debian 13.  Still, if the package was updated in SID recently I'll need to build new images to pick that up as well.  I'll assuming that's the case unless @sbrivio-rh you know otherwise.

> Force-push: Long-shot check that a rebase doesn't alter anything. If the problem is fixed in updated packages, I'll need to build fresh CI VM images.

Yes -- even better, [passt-0^20230627.g289301b-1.fc39](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2023-846daa6dbe), but the single fix included there shouldn't affect Podman's tests.

> > Note that the current package from Debian 12 is not affected as it didn't contain the offending change.
> 
> The debian CI VM images are all based on SID, so this current set bumps us up to Debian 13. Still, if the package was updated in SID recently I'll need to build new images to pick that up as well. I'll assuming that's the case unless @sbrivio-rh you know otherwise.

I just requested an upload for `0.0~git20230309.7c7625d-1` to Debian Sid (I'm a maintainer without upload rights) -- I can report on this ticket once we have a new package version there.

> I can report on this ticket once we have a new package version there.

Oh thanks, that will be really helpful.  I'm running into unrelated difficulties in https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/284 anyway, so no rush.

Force-push: New images from https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/284  These may not completely work, as there are some significant changes WRT rawhide.  However, I expect most of the int and sys tests to pass, esp. the pasta tests.
Force-push: Workaround for rawhide validation (python broken) problem.

@sbrivio-rh looks like those newer packages did the trick.  Thanks!

@rhatdan The vast majority of failures now (with images build last night) are:

```
Failed tests (3):
 - 414 podman selinux: container with label=disable
 - 415 podman selinux: privileged container
 - 420 podman selinux: pid=host
```

Am I understanding correctly the fix for those is in #18439 ?  In any case, once those tests are fixed, these new images should be good to go.

***NOTE***: [I did not tag `c20230706t200047z-f38f37d13` (which would cause renovate to propose it everywhere).](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/284#issuecomment-1625773996)  I have a somewhat mediocre level of confidence that's safe.  But they should be okay for Podman CI.

Force-push: Rebased.

***Note to me:*** IIRC Dan said it was okay to `skip(<reason>)` the failing SELinux tests.
Force-push: [Cherry-picked Dan's commit](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19376#issuecomment-1651930492).

> @sbrivio-rh I'm still seeing a `not ok 481 podman networking with pasta(1) - Local forwarder, IPv4` failure, but only on Debian SID (from ~20 days ago). [Your prior comment mentioned that you requested an update in SID](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18612#issuecomment-1609962929), to passt `0.0~git20230625.32660ce-1`. I'm seeing passt `0.0~git20230309.7c7625d-1` was installed in these Debian images cry Do you know off-hand if that package update made it in recently?

Sorry, yes, I requested an upload (I'm a Debian maintainer without upload rights), but my sponsor seems to have missed it. I'll keep you posted here.

Thanks @sbrivio-rh maybe he's on vacation or something.  [I just was able to check](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/285), and indeed it's still installing `0.0~git20230309.7c7625d-1`.  I'll wait for your word.
Force-push: testing newer images to make sure nothing new broke

@edsantiago With images built yesterday, I'm seeing a few new system test failures on rawhide:

Failed tests (3):
 - [255 podman export, alter tarball, re-import](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5491526906675200/html/sys-podman-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html)
 - 305 podman run --blkio-weight-device
 - 404 podman update - test all options

The first one `podman export, alter tarball, re-import` reproduces multiple times (root and rootless).  I didn't see an entry on your flake-list for it.  The message is:

`Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:310...9e1": processing tar file(): duplicates of file paths not supported`

Have you see this?  Any idea what could be causing it?

blkio is https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2226558

Will look at the others later.
Filed #19407 for the `tar` bug.

I'm going to assume that the [update failure](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4858208209076224/html/sys-remote-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00404) is simply a manifestation of the same loopback bug described above.

> I'm going to assume

Yeah, I didn't do any re-runs.  Thanks for the tar fix!
Force-push: Because openshift-merge-bot said so.  CI will still fail.

Force-push: I've reached my limit on this PR, and lack of F38 + updated rawhide testing at-large is hampering ongoing development and pending/imminent releases.  Added skips for all persistently failing tests, with "TODO/FIXME" comments and references where possible.

@edsantiago IIRC, you have a preferred format for these kinds of skip messages.  Please advise how you'd like them to appear.  This push is to see if we can even get a clean-run at this point (w/o new images).
You also need to skip this test in rawhide, same BZ: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/608f484e9b90e5b2589b77f2b4794168106e1e17/test/system/280-update.bats#L19
Force-push: 43rd time's the charm?

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL.  This isn't perfect, I've had to add some test skips and workarounds.  But for months worth of trying, this is the best I can get it.  The skips/workarounds can be addressed by followup PRs.  Assuming it's good, let's get this in so I can backport it to the v4.6 branch (unless there's a reason not to?).

> Thanks @sbrivio-rh maybe he's on vacation or something. [I just was able to check](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/285), and indeed it's still installing `0.0~git20230309.7c7625d-1`. I'll wait for your word.

Behold, for 'tis my word. Debian unstable (sid) package is now updated to [0.0~git20230627.289301b-1](https://packages.debian.org/source/unstable/passt).

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *edsantiago*, *renovate[bot]* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
/hold cancel
